title: House of UD
summary: Running Depano, Barber Syndicate and My Artist like a three-brand operating room.
problem: Three beauty-adjacent brands needed public worlds, creator motion and content systems without waiting for a slow agency machine.
moves: Built brand identity, content direction and rollout rhythm across Depano, Barber Syndicate and My Artist.
Owned shoots, creator coordination and campaign surfaces so each brand could feel distinct but operationally connected.
Turned fuzzy product and service ideas into content people could understand quickly in a feed.
outcomes: Two brands built from zero into visible public-facing identities.
Three-brand operating cadence across shoots, creators, content and distribution.
A repeatable brand system that can keep producing without losing taste.
proof: Verified from current CV/app data: three brands, two built from zero, active brand management role.
toneLine: This is where the design brain becomes useful: one eye on culture, one eye on commerce, one eye on how it looks in a feed at 2 AM.

title: SuperProfile x CosmoFeed
summary: A creator-growth engine pretending to be a normal marketing role.
problem: The business needed creator supply, paid activation volume, agency partnerships and market expansion to move together.
moves: Onboarded creators into a partner program built for repeatable activation.
Executed paid activations while building the agency partnership vertical.
Helped shape a USA-market play without losing the day-to-day content machine.
outcomes: 100+ creators onboarded.
500+ paid activations executed.
Agency partnerships vertical and USA-market motion launched.
proof: Verified from current CV/app data: 100+ creators, 500+ activations, agency partnerships, USA play.
toneLine: Most people make a calendar. I built a little factory where creators, offers and distribution kept colliding.

title: Burger Bae
summary: Streetwear campaign with messy internet energy and a serious commercial outcome.
problem: The brand had the raw material for attention but needed creator-led distribution that felt native, not like a polished ad pretending to be cool.
moves: Built creator-led campaign energy around the brand.
Pushed loud, platform-native content instead of sterile marketing.
Connected creator momentum to a clearer revenue story.
outcomes: Moved from roughly 30K/day to 3L/day during campaign momentum.
Created a sharper creator-led streetwear signal.
Made the brand easier to talk about, share and remember.
proof: Verified from current CV/app data: 30K/day to 3L/day campaign movement.
toneLine: Burger Bae had the correct disease: it wanted to be loud. I helped it scream in the right rooms.

title: Seekhobecho / Rawcast
summary: Built Rawcast as Seekhobecho's media vertical from zero.
problem: The brand needed a media surface that could carry long-form trust instead of only short-form fragments.
moves: Hosted and produced long-form podcast episodes.
Built the media vertical from scratch.
Turned conversations into recurring brand surface area.
outcomes: A 0-to-1 content vertical with a clear format.
Long-form proof of voice, taste and consistency.
A stronger bridge between brand narrative and audience trust.
proof: Verified from current CV/app data and linked YouTube embed.
toneLine: A podcast is not content. It is a room where a brand can stop sounding like a LinkedIn caption.

title: Baecave x Radisson
summary: A creator room inside Radisson. Not a meetup. A contained social experiment.
problem: The event needed more than attendance. It needed a room that creators would want to document, remember and distribute.
moves: Curated a creator room with 30-40 creators.
Managed the vibe, people and output together.
Designed offline energy for online distribution.
outcomes: 30-40 creators gathered in one branded environment.
Event energy converted into content surface area.
The experience felt social first, brand second, which is why it worked.
proof: Verified from current CV/app data: Baecave x Radisson, 30-40 creators.
toneLine: The best events feel slightly impossible even when every operational detail is handled.

title: InvestX / Chitkara Innovation Incubator
summary: Capital ecosystem, startup rooms and a 30-person operating crew.
problem: Startup rooms collapse when nobody owns the room, the people, the investor flow and the operating tempo together.
moves: Led InvestX operations and coordinated a 30-person team.
Supported 40+ early-stage startups.
Worked with 10+ VCs and capital-room stakeholders.
outcomes: 20CR+ in investor commitments.
40+ startups supported.
A working operating layer around founders, investors and event execution.
proof: Verified from current CV/app data: 20CR+, 40+ startups, 30-person team, 10+ VCs.
toneLine: Nobody knows what is happening, but somebody still has to run the room. I became that somebody.

title: Tongues on Fire
summary: A community built before permission to be impressive.
problem: Before credentials, the only way to prove taste and leadership was to build a room people kept returning to.
moves: Built a 120+ member community.
Managed a team of 5 and 20+ interns.
Ran sessions, events and national festival participation.
outcomes: 300+ sessions.
7 events and 2 national festivals.
Early proof of momentum, team leadership and community taste.
proof: Verified from current CV/app data: 120+ members, 20+ interns, 300+ sessions, 7 events, 2 national festivals.
toneLine: Before credentials, I had momentum. Momentum is better.
